---
title: "Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts"
chapter_id: "coil-attention-cyclic-memory-and-recurrence-contracts"
part_id: "routing-compression-representation-substrates"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "coil_attention_memory"
  - "circle_ai_contract_suite"
  - "theseus_circle_transfer"
  - "vcm_public"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `coil-attention-cyclic-memory-and-recurrence-contracts` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Representation, and Substrates |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `coil_attention_memory`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer`, `vcm_public` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Memory, attention, and recurrence mechanisms need finite structural contracts for aliasing, coverage, freshness, active work, loop exits, and overthinking boundaries.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Long context, sparse attention, ring buffers, and recursive loops can silently hide stale reads, uncovered lags, duplicate slots, alias collisions, or unbounded work.

## Core Claim

[coil-attention-cyclic-memory-and-recurrence-contracts.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Coil attention and memory contracts should be used as structural guardrails for cyclic memory, sparse coverage, recurrence schedules, and work-budget admission, not as claims of retrieval or reasoning quality.

## Mechanism

- Map memory slots, residue, winding, strides, windows, and recurrence schedules into finite circular structures.
- Expose alias, freshness, coverage, active-token, work-saving, and loop-exit fields as auditable contract outputs.
- Compare slot-only, slot-plus-winding, FIFO, LRU, content-gated, and ordinary attention or memory baselines before promoting performance claims.

## Interfaces

- VCM context traces expose context adequacy and authority labels.
- Attention and memory modules consume contract fields as diagnostic guardrails.
- Benchmark adapters and Theseus transfer lanes decide whether structural facts survive actual workloads.
- Proof contracts define the finite boundary of the claim.

## Invariants

- Residue-only aliasing remains visible.
- Full structural coverage is not semantic coverage.
- Recurrence budgets include exits and guardrails.
- Stale reads fail closed or enter residual escrow.

## Failure modes

- Alias hiding.
- Overthinking or non-terminating recurrence loops.
- Uncovered sparse-attention lags.
- Freshness facts treated as retrieval-quality evidence.
- No ordinary baseline controls.

## Minimal implementation

A contract-backed memory and attention evaluation plan with slot, winding, coverage, freshness, active-token, loop-exit, work-budget, and baseline fields.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Cyclic alias visibility test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Sparse coverage gap test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Recurrence budget and exit test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Freshness stale-read rejection test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `coil_attention_memory` | Coil Attention and Memory | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Proof-linked cyclic memory, KV-cache freshness, sparse-attention coverage, recurrence schedules, loop-exit certificates, work budgets, and alias diagnostics. |
| `circle_ai_contract_suite` | Circle Calculus AI Contract Suite | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Theorem-linked AI contract families for RoPE, KV-cache freshness, sparse attention, recurrence schedules, strided fanout, cyclic memory, multicoil phase, cyclic mixers, and seed-rule regeneration. |
| `theseus_circle_transfer` | Theseus Circle Calculus Transfer Lane | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Report-only bridge from Circle finite fixtures into private Theseus benchmark design with explicit quality/runtime/memory/transfer/failure-case claim boundaries. |
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
